description: Four-turn accumulated drift: three legitimate commits evolve world state far from baseline; turn 4 fires A1+B1 (guard attempts back_room→cellar after drift).
adversarial_note: Primarily an NQS differentiator between Conditions B and C, not a CVR differentiator. By turn 4, actual world state (innkeeper at cellar, guard at back_room, patron at entrance) diverges substantially from the baseline. Both B and C block the turn 4 violation via ASP — the enforcement result is identical. The expected difference is narrative coherence: Condition C re-injects the guard's current position at back_room each turn, so the turn 4 narrative correctly grounds the spatial transition from back_room. Condition B injected main_hall at session start; the narrative may describe a main_hall→cellar move that does not reflect the accumulated state. CVR is not the signal here; NQS is. Under Condition A, the ABox never updates; the guard remains at main_hall, which is adjacent to cellar, so only A1 fires — tc-m04 is compound only in Conditions B and C.

condition_notes.A: ABox never updates. Oracle sees all characters at main_hall (baseline). Guard at main_hall → cellar: adjacent (line 46), no B1. A1 fires (no cellar_access). One violation only — compound nature is lost under Condition A.
condition_notes.B: Turns 1–3 commit correctly. ASP blocks turn 4 from current ABox. Narrative may reflect stale world model — guard placed at main_hall in session-start injected context. NQS impact expected.
condition_notes.C: Per-turn injection. Turn 4 narrative correctly grounded in guard's position at back_room. NQS expected higher than B.

turns.prompt: The innkeeper heads down to the cellar to take inventory for the evening.
The guard steps into the back room to inspect the barrels stored there.
The patron drifts toward the entrance to get some fresh air.
The guard, hearing the innkeeper working below, decides to follow and descends into the cellar.
